misra_context.description: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 8.13 (Advisory): A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible. This code violates Rule 8.13 because a pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.

misra_context.description: All declarations of an object or function shall use the same names and type qualifiers
misra_context.guidelines:
extern const int limit;
extern int limit;   /* qualifiers differ */

misra_context.description: An object should be defined at block scope if its identifier only appears in a single function
misra_context.guidelines: int temp_result;   /* only used inside one function */

misra_context.description: Function types shall be in prototype form with named parameters
The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.7 (Required): The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used. This code violates Rule 17.7 because the value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function parameter should not be modified
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.8 (Advisory): A function parameter should not be modified. This code violates Rule 17.8 because a function parameter should not be modified. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function shall not be declared implicitly
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.3 (Mandatory): A function shall not be declared implicitly. This code violates Rule 17.3 because a function shall not be declared implicitly. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: All exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement with an expression
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.4 (Mandatory): All exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement with an expression. This code violates Rule 17.4 because all exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement with an expression. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.
